description: A unique identifier assigned to the resource which is ResourceId, for internal tracking and reference within CPALMS.
A list of Florida B.E.S.T. Math (or other subject) benchmarks associated with the resource. These benchmarks outline specific standards the resource aligns with.
Detailed descriptions of the benchmarks linked to the resource, specifying the skill or concept being taught along with relevant Benchmark IDs.
The name or title of the resource, providing a brief descriptor of its content or activity.
A brief summary or overview of the resource’s content or purpose. This helps educators understand what the resource provides and how it supports learning.
The primary form of information and communication technology (ICT) used in the resource. For example, this could refer to worksheets, apps, or other digital tools.
Any special materials or tools required to use the resource effectively. This field may be left null if no special materials are needed.
Links to any downloadable resources or files related to the lesson or activity. In this case, it refers to a downloadable 'double ten frame' worksheet.
A list of unique Benchmark IDs related to the resource. These are used to cross-reference the resource with specific Florida B.E.S.T. benchmarks.
The grade levels for which the resource is designed, indicating the intended age group or academic stage (e.g., K for kindergarten, 1 for first grade).
The subject area(s) associated with the resource, such as Mathematics, Science, Social Studies, etc.
The primary audience for the resource, such as 'Educators,' 'Students,' or specific groups like 'Parents' or 'Administrators.'
The URL where the resource can be accessed or previewed on the CPALMS website. This link allows educators to view or download the resource directly.
The date and time when the resource was published on CPALMS. This helps track when the resource became available to educators.
An identifier for the type of resource (e.g., lesson plan, activity, worksheet). Each resource type is associated with a unique ID.
An identifier for the primary ICT (Information and Communication Technology) resource type, linked to the resource. This helps classify the technology or media used.
Information about any accommodations provided for students with special learning needs. This field may be left null if accommodations are not specified.
Opportunities or suggestions for extending the learning, such as advanced activities, additional resources, or ways to adapt the lesson for different learning needs.
Additional recommendations for educators on how to best implement the resource in the classroom, or suggestions for related activities or follow-up lessons.
Clearly defined educational goals or targets that outline what students should know and be able to do by the end of the lesson.
A secondary reading passage used in the lesson to support comprehension or provide additional context to the lesson's main topic.
Questions posed by the teacher to direct student focus, provoke thought, and guide them through the key concepts of the lesson.
The primary reading passage assigned for the lesson to help students engage with the content and stimulate discussion.
The foundational information or concepts that students should already know before beginning the lesson in order to understand the new content.
The concluding part of the lesson where the teacher helps students synthesize the information learned, check for understanding, and link it to future lessons or activities.
The segment of the lesson where the teacher delivers the content, explains the concepts, and models tasks or strategies for students.
Questions designed to assess if students are prepared to engage with the lesson's content, ensuring they have the necessary prior knowledge.
Activities or exercises in which the teacher provides support and guidance while students begin to practice the new skills or concepts.
An opportunity for students to apply and expand on their understanding by solving more complex problems or creating real-world connections.
Activities or exercises that allow students to work on their own to reinforce and apply what they’ve learned during the lesson.
Questions that encourage students to reflect on their learning, helping them make connections and evaluate their understanding of the material.
The opening activity or event designed to capture students' attention, spark curiosity, and introduce them to the lesson’s topic or concept.
Activities where students actively investigate the lesson’s concepts through hands-on tasks or collaborative work.
The phase where the teacher clarifies the concepts, provides direct instruction, and ensures students understand the key ideas being taught.
The beginning of the lesson where the teacher sets the stage, introduces the topic, and outlines what will be learned.
Opportunities for students to explore the content further through experiments, problem-solving, or inquiry-based activities.
Activities or discussions where students break down information, identify patterns, or critically assess the content they are learning.
A specific template or format for writing a letter as part of the lesson’s task or communication activity.
A collection of data provided for analysis or interpretation during the lesson, typically used in experiments or research tasks.
Another set of data that may be used in the lesson for comparison, contrast, or further analysis to deepen student understanding.
Ongoing assessments that occur during the lesson to monitor students' understanding and guide future instruction.
Questions that help guide students' thinking and reflection during or after the lesson to deepen their understanding of the material.
A second letter template used for communication activities or to structure students' written tasks during the lesson.
Any extra instructions or materials needed to support the lesson or enhance the learning experience for students.
Suggestions for the teacher on how to approach the lesson, including strategies for managing the classroom and supporting diverse learners.
Questions designed to assess students' prior knowledge and readiness for the new lesson content.
Additional reading materials or resources provided to reinforce or extend the lesson's content.
Specific feedback given to students on their work, responses, or progress to help them improve and reflect on their learning.
An evaluation or assessment at the end of the lesson or unit to gauge the students' overall understanding and mastery of the material.
Notes that align the lesson with the Florida B.E.S.T. (Benchmarks for Excellent Student Thinking) standards to ensure the content meets state educational requirements.
An activity where students make predictions based on prior knowledge or observations, helping to engage them in the lesson.
An activity or phase where students observe phenomena, experiments, or examples to gather data or insights related to the lesson's concept.
A step-by-step outline of the actions students will take to complete a task, experiment, or learning activity.
Notes or tips for the teacher, often related to managing the lesson, addressing student needs, or extending the content.
Activities, materials, or components that are not essential but can be used if time permits or to enrich the lesson.
Strategies and methods used to ensure that all students, including those with diverse learning needs, can access, engage with, and demonstrate understanding of the lesson's content.

field: CTE Course Info and Benchmark Notes
description: Information related to Career and Technical Education (CTE) courses, including course objectives, relevant benchmarks, and any additional instructional or assessment guidelines.
Activities or discussions that link lesson content to real-world career applications, helping students see the relevance of the material in various professions.
An outline that provides an overview of the unit's structure, including major topics, learning objectives, and how the lesson fits into the broader unit context.
